faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Carle Place home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small holes in woolens, silk, or fur items; webbing in closet corners; tiny beige or gold moths flying near wardrobes; and casings or larvae in fabric folds. You may also notice damage to carpets, upholstery, or stored blankets. Our Carle Place technicians provide thorough inspections to identify the extent of infestation.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth control cost in Carle Place?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control in Carle Place typically ranges from $550 to $5,000 depending on the severity of infestation, size of your home, and number of treatments needed. Minor infestations in single rooms cost less, while whole-home treatments for severe cases require more extensive service. We provide free estimates and customize treatment plans to fit your specific situation and budget.

Our local Long Island teams can quickly reach any Carle Place location to begin treatment and stop the infestation from spreading.

faqs.question: How can I prevent clothes moths from returning after treatment?
faqs.answer: Prevention includes regular cleaning and vacuuming of closets, storing woolens and natural fabrics in sealed containers, using cedar blocks or lavender sachets as natural deterrents, and maintaining low humidity levels. Our Carle Place technicians provide detailed prevention guidance and offer follow-up inspections to ensure your home remains moth-free. We also recommend periodic professional treatments for long-term protection.
